attributes.name: level
attributes.description: On <auto-heading>: explicit heading level (e.g. "3"); highest-priority source for the resolved <hN> tag, clamped to a maximum of 6.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: On <smart-link>: when present the element resolves to <a> (navigation); when absent it resolves to <button> (action).

properties.name: excludedAttributes
properties.description: Gets the list of attribute names to exclude from transfer to the replacement element (defaults to an empty array).

slots.description: Default slot: child nodes are moved (not cloned) to the replacement element, preserving listeners on children.

name: Transient Component
summary: Base class and utilities for custom elements that replace themselves with semantically correct native HTML during connectedCallback — enabling context-aware element selection without permanent wrapper nodes.

replaceWith.pattern: Element.replaceWith() replaces an element in its parent's child list with new nodes
replaceWith.adoption: Core mechanism — transient element calls queueMicrotask(() => this.replaceWith(replacement)) from connectedCallback to safely self-replace after callback stack completes

customElements.pattern: Autonomous custom elements with connectedCallback lifecycle
customElements.adoption: Transient elements are autonomous custom elements that run initialization logic in connectedCallback, then replace themselves with native elements

queueMicrotask.pattern: Schedules a microtask to run after current script completes but before rendering
queueMicrotask.adoption: Defers replaceWith() out of connectedCallback stack to avoid re-entrancy with disconnectedCallback

headingElements.pattern: <h1>–<h6> provide document heading hierarchy; WHATWG outline algorithm removed from spec
headingElements.adoption: AutoHeading replaces itself with the correct <hN> element based on nesting depth or injector-provided level

ariaHeadingRole.pattern: role='heading' with aria-level for non-native heading semantics
ariaHeadingRole.adoption: Fallback only — MDN recommends native <hN> elements over role='heading'. Self-replacement produces native elements.

microtaskDeferral.description: Self-replacement uses queueMicrotask() to defer replaceWith() outside the connectedCallback stack
microtaskDeferral.rationale: Calling replaceWith() synchronously inside connectedCallback triggers disconnectedCallback re-entrantly. The microtask runs after connectedCallback completes but before paint — zero visual flash, clean lifecycle.

attributeTransfer.description: All attributes except internal ones (is, data-transient-*) are transferred to the replacement element
attributeTransfer.rationale: Authors expect class, id, style, aria-* and custom attributes to carry over. The transient element is a stand-in, not a wrapper.

childNodeTransfer.description: All child nodes are moved to the replacement element via append(...this.childNodes)
childNodeTransfer.rationale: Children belong to the semantic target, not the transient host. Moving (not cloning) preserves event listeners on children.

eventListenerWarning.description: Event listeners attached to the transient element itself are lost on replacement. This is by design.
eventListenerWarning.rationale: Transient elements are meant to be replaced before user interaction. Listeners should be on children (which survive) or on the replacement (added post-replacement via event delegation).

noCustomizedBuiltins.description: Does NOT use customized built-in elements (is='' attribute / extends: 'h1')
noCustomizedBuiltins.rationale: Safari refuses to implement customized built-ins and has stated it never will. The polyfill is unreliable. Autonomous custom elements that self-replace are the portable alternative.

injectorHeadingLevel.description: AutoHeading resolves heading level from InjectorRoot ('headingLevel' provider) first, falls back to DOM traversal (counting sectioning ancestors)
injectorHeadingLevel.rationale: InjectorRoot mirrors React's HeadingLevelContext pattern — a section component increments the level in its injector scope. All descendants read the correct level without DOM traversal. Fallback to DOM traversal covers cases without explicit section contexts.

ssrStrategy.description: SSR must compute the correct element server-side (e.g., Eleventy shortcode). Client-side transient element upgrades the fallback.
ssrStrategy.rationale: Custom elements require JS to connectedCallback. Crawlers and screen readers see the pre-upgrade HTML. The server must emit the correct <hN> for accessibility and SEO. The client-side element is progressive enhancement.

react.notes: React's context-based heading level is the canonical reference; components render the correct JSX element directly

angular.notes: ng-container removes itself at compile time — different mechanism but same goal (no wrapper node)

vue.notes: Dynamic component tag selection; template element renders no DOM

svelte.notes: Dynamic element tag at compile time; no runtime wrapper node

traits.name: withSelfReplacement
traits.description: Base mixin that handles the microtask-deferred replaceWith() lifecycle, attribute transfer, and child node migration
